issue.body:
KRL doesn't allow name/value bindings except in a prelude or in a function definition. When these are tried in other places, the error messages are unclear/misleading and sometimes don't fail until JavaScript compilation. Need specific examples. But, see also, issue #528, which makes a case for such bindings in an `every` action block.

KRL doesn't allow entity variable assignments anywhere but in a postlude. When one is attempted in a `defaction` the error message seen is "ParseError: Expected '('" which is not helpful.
issue.title: Need a better parser/compile error message for mis-placed bindings/assignments

comments.body:
One reported error is, «it's saying "SyntaxError: Unexpected token 'const'" but there's no 'const' in my code anywhere». 

Melissa Robertson says, "I’ve run into that before, it means there is a syntax error in your code, but the parser isn’t picking up on it so it doesn’t fail until it starts generating the javascript under the hood".

Phil says, "The bug you uncovered is that a decl should never be allowed in the postlude. So, this should be illegal:

```
always {
  x = 3
}
```
You can set an entity variable, but you can't bind names to values in the postlude.

So, the parser should have caught this, but didn't. Then the compiler generated illegal JavaScript, causing a JS parse error, which isn't helpful.

We'll be updating the parser to disallow decls in postludes to avoid this."

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
- The pico-engine packages have all been updated to run on the latest Node.js
- Only breaking change effects direct consumers of pico-framework or pico-engine-core. [Described here](https://github.com/Picolab/pico-framework/pull/1#issue-1989174232)
- Updated the github actions to ensure tests will run against the LTS and Current node versions on Linux/Mac/Windows.
- Most of the npm packages have been updated. There are some exceptions outlined below.

## What changed by package

- [select-when](https://github.com/Picolab/select-when)
  - No changes to the user
  - Updated typescript, setup testing
  - Published 0.1.9
- [pico-framework](https://github.com/Picolab/pico-framework)
  - Only breaking change to how the database is configured. [Described here](https://github.com/Picolab/pico-framework/pull/1#issue-1989174232)
    - This only effects those who consume pico-framework directly
  - Updated dependencies minus `cuid` (see below)
  - Setup testing
  - Published 0.7.0
- krl-compiler
  - No changes to the user
  - Updated dependencies minus `standard` (see below)
- krl-generator
  - No changes to the user
  - Updated dependencies minus `standard` (see below)
- krl-parser
  - No changes to the user
  - Updated dependencies
- krl-stdlib
  - No changes to the user
  - Updated dependencies
- pico-engine-core
  - Breaking change in how the db is configured. Same change as pico-framework.
    - You pass in a leveldb that meets the PicoDb api rather than a leveldown instance.
    - This only effects those who consume pico-core directly 
  - Updated dependencies minus `cuid,bs58,normalize-url,p-memoize,random-words` (see below)
- pico-engine-ui
  - Switched from [Webpack](https://webpack.js.org/) to [Vite](https://vitejs.dev/)
    - It's a newer alternative that is much simpler to configure, and faster
    - Switching to this was easier than update all the webpack depencies
    - No change to development workflow.
  - Updated dependencies including React 18
    - NOTE: Moved bootstrap from npm to a simple .min.css file
       - The new scss compiler doesn't like bootstrap v4's scss code.
       - Since we don't customize bootstrap there's no need to leave it in npm.
       - Upgrading to v5 does not seem [worth it.](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.0/migration/)
- pico-engine
  - No changes to the user.
  - Updated dependencies minus `cuid` and `rotating-file-stream` (see below)

## Dependencies not updated

```
Package               Current  Wanted  Latest  Location                           Depended by
lerna                  3.22.1  3.22.1   8.0.0  node_modules/lerna                 root
node-gyp                9.4.1   9.4.1  10.0.1  node_modules/node-gyp              root
standard               12.0.1  12.0.1  17.1.0  node_modules/standard              krl-compiler
standard               12.0.1  12.0.1  17.1.0  node_modules/standard              krl-generator
cuid                    2.1.8   2.1.8   3.0.0  node_modules/cuid                  pico-engine
rotating-file-stream    1.4.6   1.4.6   3.1.1  node_modules/rotating-file-stream  pico-engine
bs58                    4.0.1   4.0.1   5.0.0  node_modules/bs58                  pico-engine-core
cuid                    2.1.8   2.1.8   3.0.0  node_modules/cuid                  pico-engine-core
normalize-url           5.3.1   5.3.1   8.0.0  node_modules/normalize-url         pico-engine-core
p-memoize               4.0.4   4.0.4   7.1.1  node_modules/p-memoize             pico-engine-core
random-words            1.3.0   1.3.0   2.0.0  node_modules/random-words          pico-engine-core
```

- lerna
  - The project has significant changes, and not wroth updating at this time
  - This doesn't effect consumers of the pico-engine
  - npm now has workspaces, so it's possible we could migrate off of lerna
- node-gyp
  - I left this as is since I don't know why it's needed on the root package.json
- [standard](https://standardjs.com/)
  - This is the linter/formatter used on krl-compiler and krl-generator. Only used for development.
  - Upgrading this would require re-formatting all the files. I don't think it's worth it at this time since it'd be so noisy.
  - Ideally we'd move away from standard and use prettier like the other packages.
- [cuid](https://github.com/paralleldrive/cuid#status-deprecated-due-to-security-use-cuid2-instead)
   - We use this to create unique ids for channels among other things.
   - cuid's include a timestamp and machine thumbprint
   - The maintainer deprecated cuid since the id's can be guessed, therefore insecure
   - Changing this could be significant b/c I'm not sure if people depend on the structure of the ids to include timestamps and sortability.
- [rotating-file-stream](https://www.npmjs.com/package/rotating-file-stream)
  - I'm not as familiar with how pico-engine logs are used in the real world.
  - The package has good documentation on what was changed for [v2](https://github.com/iccicci/rotating-file-stream#upgrading-from-v1-to-v2) and [v3](https://github.com/iccicci/rotating-file-stream#upgrading-from-v2-to-v3)
  - Leaving it as is works fine on the latest Nodejs
- [bs58](https://github.com/cryptocoinjs/bs58/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md)
  - They changed it to use Uint8Array
  - I'm not as familiar with the ursa and dido krl modules, so I opted to leave it alone.
- [normalize-url](https://www.npmjs.com/package/normalize-url), [p-memoize](https://www.npmjs.com/package/p-memoize), [random-words](https://www.npmjs.com/package/random-words)
  - All of these require moving to the new ESModule standard
  - The annoying thing is this would require converting our packages to [ESM](https://gist.github.com/sindresorhus/a39789f98801d908bbc7ff3ecc99d99c) which changes how users consume it.

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
Adding new syntax for raise statements that allow a dynamic expression for the event domain

Original statement:
`raise <word> event <expression> [for <expression>] [attributes <expression>]`

New additional syntax:
`raise event <expression> [for <expression>] [attributes <expression>]`

For example:
```js
raise foo event "bar"
// - or -
raise event "foo:bar"
```

`raise event <expression> : <expression>` and `raise event <expression> <expression>` both had parser ambiguities, that's why there is a single expression for both the domain and type.

The domain-type expression is evaluated to a string (using KRL's toString logic) then the domain and type are parsed out separated by a `:`
pull_requests_reviewed.title: dynamic raise statement - resolves #15

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
Originally I went to resolve #439, since it's a slight breaking change, I thought it would be good to fix several other similar stdlib inconsistencies in one batch.

The following functions now work on both Arrays and Maps. It throws an error if it's used on anything else. Also it will throw an error if the iterator is not a function.

- `.all`
- `.any`
- `.collect`
- `.filter`
- `.map`
- `.index` (also index allows you to pass an optional equality function)
- `.reduce` (also reduce returns null if no default is given)

The following now throw an error if the value is not an array.

- `.slice`
- `.splice`
- `.sort`
- `.has`
- `.once`
- `.duplicates`
- `.unique`

`.head` Works on Arrays and Strings. i.e. `"one".head() == "o"` If not an array or string return null

`.tail` Works on Arrays and Strings i.e. `"one".tail() == "ne"` If not an array or string return []

Non-breaking changes:
Ability to set flags on a regex i.e. `.as("RegExp", "gi")` see #431 


<!---
@huboard:{"custom_state":"archived"}
-->

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
#375 This pull request has a fix for this issue.

Changes:

- When a new child pico is created, rulesets are installed using the `install_rulesets_requested` rule for any user-requested rulesets to be installed on that child. OS rulesets (e.g. wrangler, visual_params) are still installed at the `createChild` defaction in wrangler. `child_initialized` is not sent back to the parent until any user-requested rulesets have been successful or failed in installation.
- Install rulesets rule now handles invalid rids, checking if an rid is registered with the engine before attempting install. If a given rid is not registered with the engine, wrangler will raise a `install_rulesets_error` event with attributes containing the names of the invalid rulesets. If no valid rids are given or no rids are given a system/error even is raised giving relevant information. 
- Added a new proxy rule to facilitate installing rulesets before sending a `child_initialized` event 


- Subscriptions automatically installed on child picos
pull_requests_reviewed.title: Install Rulesets Validation

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
Seven seconds after the engine starts a discovery service is started.
discover service allows you to add resources to be advertised to other pico engines on the network.  discover service also allows you to register observer DiD's. when discover finds another engine, it will send an event to all the observer dids with what that engine is advertising. This enables any observer to listen for different types of resources and the ability to subscribe to them.
The dependency has not been added. you will need to install node-discover manually
pull_requests_reviewed.title: basic engine discovery.

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
**Wrangler API changes**
Added createPolicy and createChannel defactions in wrangler and provided them.

**Subscription API changes**
outbound_bus_removed --> outbound_subscription_cancelled
inbound_bus_removed --> inbound_subscription_cancelled
subscription_added from the outbound pico used to return attributes with "_Tx", "_Tx_verify_key", and "_Tx_public_key", but the leading "_" has been removed to keep API consistent. The attr "bus" with the value being the newly approved subscription has also been added to remain consistent between the inbound and outbound subscription_added events.

**Bug Fixes**
Event attributes are propagated throughout the subscription event flow.
Cancel subscription by Id now works
Subscription Id correctly propagates in the event attrs internally.

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
This fixes #358 and #359

Syntax change
 `select when ... where .. setting() ` to `select when ... setting() where ..`
It's backwards comparable and emits a deprecation warning.

The variables in `setting()` are now available in `where`

The values in the global scope are now available in `where`. For backwards compatibility, `where` still lets you access event attributes as bare words.




<!---
@huboard:{"custom_state":"archived"}
-->
pull_requests_reviewed.title: Select where

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
What spurred this on was:
```krl
select when a a mileage re#(.*)# setting(m)
if m > 200 then
  ...
```
**>** was not very friendly about the attribute being a string.

I changed it so all comparison operators now try to convert both arguments to a number. If one or both fail to convert, then it converts them to strings and compares that.
```js
"300" > "200" is the same as  300  > 200
"300" >  200  is the same as  300  > 200
"wat" >  200  is the same as "wat" > "200"
 noop >  200  is the same as "[Function]" > "200"
```

I also changed the logic for `.as("Number")`
```js
null.as("Number") -> 0

false.as("Number") -> 0
true.as("Number") -> 1

"200".as("Number") -> 200
"wat".as("Number") -> null  // if the string cannot be parsed into a number, return null

// arrays and maps return their size
[].as("Number") -> 0
["a", "b"].as("Number") -> 2
{"a": a, "b": b, "c": c}.as("Number") -> 3

// other types return null
re#foo#.as("Number") -> null
send_directive.as("Number") -> null
```
These same number conversion rules are used everywhere.

Another change I made was making `.substr()`, `.slice()`, and `.splice()` more forgiving by return an empty string/array instead of throwing an error when the range is out of bounds. i.e.
```js
"foo".substr(1000) -> "" // instead of throwing a RangeError
```

Finally, division by zero. In many situations 0 is an acceptable response. So instead of breaking and throwing an error, it now just returns 0 and emits a debug message. i.e.
```js
9 / 0  -> 0 // and emits "[DIVISION BY ZERO] 9 / 0"
```

<!---
@huboard:{"custom_state":"archived"}
-->
pull_requests_reviewed.title: Weak typing

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
This pull renames io.picolabs.pico to io.picolabs.wrangler. 
Wrangler functions have been cleaned up to return raw requested data instead of wrapping in an unnecessary data structure. Directives in rules have been simplified as well.
Wrangler rule select statements no longer support pico has a domain. The User Interface has been updated to use wrangler domain instead of pico, there are no test to guarantee all cases have been changed. The engine now installs io.picolabs.wrangler in root instead of io.picolabs.pico.
Subscriptions have been refactored to provide more concise code with a simpler data structure and API. Subscriptions now support different level of complexity of subscriptions.
tests.js has been updated to properly use io.picolabs.wrangler and io.picolabs.subscriptions.
Subscription tests have been restructured to query subscriptions ruleset instead of scraping the database.
Changes found in other code is to support the above changes.
No changes in this pull request has been documented yet. 

<!---
@huboard:{"custom_state":"archived"}
-->

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
Problem: https://picolabsio.slack.com/messages/C07PMJ95X/

[Here](https://github.com/Picolab/pico-engine/blob/23e844e5a5db1b30cd0610f7c9084021527e99aa/packages/pico-engine/src/startCore.js#L172), `err` is not a a string, so [`logEntry`'s `needAttributes`](https://github.com/Picolab/pico-engine/blob/23e844e5a5db1b30cd0610f7c9084021527e99aa/packages/pico-engine/src/startCore.js#L95) tries to call the undefined `startsWith` function on the `message` object.

Possible solution, as in this PR: Assume `err` is either a message or an error, like in the second (burdettadam's) case I think is caused by an [unhandled](https://github.com/Picolab/pico-engine/blob/23e844e5a5db1b30cd0610f7c9084021527e99aa/packages/pico-engine-core/src/modules/engine.js#L12) [LevelDown error](https://github.com/Picolab/pico-engine/blob/cb95bcc0c08fe840a4fa469ec9ff0a4058cbdb94/packages/pico-engine-core/src/DB.js#L122).

Should we add tests to https://github.com/Picolab/pico-engine/blob/master/packages/pico-engine-core/src/DB.test.js and https://github.com/Picolab/pico-engine/blob/master/packages/pico-engine/src/tests.js ? I'm thinking of mocking a similar situation for https://github.com/Picolab/pico-engine/blob/master/packages/pico-engine-core/src/modules/engine.test.js

<!---
@huboard:{"order":0.24884025679052188,"milestone_order":318,"custom_state":""}
-->

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
Copied from an old [PR](https://github.com/Picolab/node-pico-engine/pull/24) opened by https://github.com/farskipper:

Changes
 * use the krl `.encode()` for json serialization (i.e. regex will output "re#...#")
 * prety-printing the log context

Example output:

```
[EPISODE_START] cj3ospydz0003dudxgnhg59ep
[DEBUG] event/cj3ospydv0002dudxxmtaa7mm/19/pico/root_created | event received: pico/root_created
[DEBUG] event/cj3ospydv0002dudxxmtaa7mm/19/pico/root_created | event added to pico queue: cj3ospydu0001dudxq3jnc6pz
[DEBUG] event/cj3ospydv0002dudxxmtaa7mm/19/pico/root_created | event being processed
[DEBUG] event/cj3ospydv0002dudxxmtaa7mm/19/pico/root_created | rule added to schedule: io.picolabs.pico -> pico_root_created
[DEBUG] event/cj3ospydv0002dudxxmtaa7mm/19/pico/root_created | rule selected: io.picolabs.pico -> pico_root_created
[DEBUG] event/cj3ospydv0002dudxxmtaa7mm/19/pico/root_created | fired
[DEBUG] event/cj3ospydv0002dudxxmtaa7mm/19/pico/root_created | event finished processing
[DEBUG] event/cj3ospydv0002dudxxmtaa7mm/19/pico/root_created | {"directives":[]}
[EPISODE_STOP] cj3ospydz0003dudxgnhg59ep
...
...
...
[DEBUG] query/cj3m5tcwl0002r1dxwxr18ci2/io.picolabs.logging/__testing | query received: io.picolabs.logging/__testing
```
